name: South-of-The-Border Squash
recipeIngredient: 1 cup monterey jack cheese (4 oz.), cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the yellow squash into 1/4-inch slices.
2. In a saucepan, combine the first four ingredients: squash, onion, salt, and water. Cook for 6-8 minutes or until the squash is tender. Drain well.
3. Add chopped green chiles, pimiento, and cubed monterey jack cheese to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
4. Spoon the mixture into a lightly greased 1 3/4-quart casserole dish.
5. Place sliced boiled eggs on top of the squash mixture (optional).
6. Sprinkle crushed corn chips evenly over the top of the casserole.
7. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes or until the casserole is thoroughly heated.
